mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 14.7% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines below 14.7% without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signal accommodative policy amid rising price pressures, creating a 'stagflationary' environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.9 and FEDFUNDS is below 0.05, or the correlation between proxies and returns falls below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate robust demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 60d returns in > 70% of instances where both HOUST exceeds 1216 and RSAFS is above 403946, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances despite declining INDPRO, or the coefficient on INDPRO in regression models falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening labor market that boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances with PAYEMS growth above 131045, or the predictive power of PAYEMS declines below 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1216 units signals weakening demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investor sentiment deteriorates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances with HOUST below 1216, or the correlation between HOUST and returns falls below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 60d returns in > 70% of instances where both GDPC1 exceeds 18987.877 and HOUST is above 1216, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.10).
